description: The Paris Court of Appeal opened an investigation into the former head of the EU Border agency on March 24 for potentially participating in crimes against humanity. The former border chief, Fabrice Leggeri resigned in 2022 following allegations of mistreatment of migrants at EU borders. Now a lawmaker for France's far-right National Rally party, he denies the allegations.

title: Heritage sites in the Middle East under threat due to conflict
description: The Middle East war is having an impact on the region's heritage sites. Archaeological and Unesco heritage sites have been collateral damage in the war, on all sides. Unesco believes at least 6 heritage sites in the Middle East have been damaged, the Tyre site in Lebanon, four in Iran and the White City in Israel. In Iran, the historic city of isfahan and the Golestan palace in Tehran were also damaged.

title: Iran 'dismisses' US 15-point peace plan, issues its own ceasefire proposal
description: Iran on March 25 an American plan to pause the war in the Middle East, issuing its own counterproposal instead, as it launched more attacks on Israel and Gulf Arab countries. Its own plan includes a halt to killings of its officials, means to make sure no other war is waged against it, reparations for the war, the end of hostilities and Iran’s “exercise of sovereignty over the Strait of Hormuz.” FRANCE 24's Philip Turle takes a look.

title: Emblematic MeToo trial of Islamic scholar Tariq Ramadan awaits verdict
description: Tariq Ramadan, a well-known Swiss-Egyptian Islamic scholar, has been tried in absentia for the rape of three women in Paris, with the verdict to fall on March 25. The trial is one of the most emblematic of the MeToo movement in France. He has already been convicted in Switzerland, where he is currently hospitalised for multiple sclerosis - which his medical team judged made him unable to attend his trial in France. FRANCE 24's Angela Diffley look

title: Ukrainian drones crash in Estonia and Latvia, raising air defence concerns
description: Stray Ukrainian drones hit Estonia and Latvia early on March 25, one of them slamming into a chimney at a power station. There were no reports of injuries in the incidents, and the drones were believed to be part of a wider Ukrainian attack on Russia targeting energy infrastructure. Latvia says these drone incursions could keep happening in the Baltic region.

title: Iran, Nigeria, and Africa’s oil dependency
description: Could this be the moment Africa breaks its dependency on foreign energy?

title: War on Iran: What troops is the US moving to the Gulf?
description: Growing US military presence around Iran suggests shift from air campaign to potential ground operations.

title: Trump Says He’s Talking With Iran. Iran Says He’s Not. Here’s Why.
description: The president has domestic and international political motivations for touting negotiations to end the war. Iran has similar ones to deny discussions.

title: What it would take to end the Iran war
description: The termination of the US-Israeli war on Iran would require solid guarantees and a credible guarantor.

title: Is war turning Tehran’s air into a chemical weapon?
description: Black rain follows Tehran oil strikes, spreading toxic fallout into air, soil and water.

title: Investigators Seek Answers in Attacks on Jewish Sites in Europe
description: Attacks on schools and property in several countries have Jewish communities on edge, amid suspicions that Iran is behind the violence.

title: U.S. and Iran Are Talking About Ways to End the War: What to Know
description: The United States is discussing ways to end hostilities with Iran, even as President Trump continues to threaten attacks and as the Pentagon dispatches more troops.

title: Iran War Live Updates: Tehran Dismisses Trump’s Cease-Fire Offer but Signals Openness to Talks
description: The Pentagon is sending more troops to the region even as President Trump seemed to endorse Pakistan’s offer to broker negotiations.

title: Правительство Израиля разрешило армии призвать до 400 тысяч резервистов
description: Правительство Израиля одобрило план призвать в войска до 400 тысяч резервистов в связи с военными действиями против Ирана и "Хезболлы", пишет The Times of Israel со ссылкой на заявление Армии обороны Израиля.

title: Iran appears to target Israeli power plant in missile attack, but misses
description:
Senior Iranian official confirms receipt of US peace proposal, with Turkey and Pakistan possible venues for talks; IAF bombs Iran's sole facility for developing submarines

title: What the Iran War reveals about missile warfare - Sponsored Content
description:
Tal Inbar breaks down why cluster munitions are so difficult to intercept, how Israel’s air defense systems are performing, and what it all means for the future of Israeli strategy.
The post What the Iran War reveals about missile warfare appeared first on The Times of Israel.

title: With war keeping customers and workers away, retailers struggle to keep doors open
description:
As missiles rain down and schools remain closed, many small business owners are reducing hours or staying shut altogether, while those that open wonder if there is any point
The post With war keeping customers and workers away, retailers struggle to keep doors open appeared first on The Times of Israel.

title: Seeking a deal with different faces of the same regime, Trump risks subverting his own vital war goals
description:
When the bombs stop, he promised Iranians, your government 'will be yours to take.' But it won't be. And as for his talk of potential 'guaranteed peace for Israel' - well, would that it were so
The post Seeking a deal with different faces of the same regime, Trump risks subverting his own vital war goals appeared first on The Times of Israel.

title: Republican Sen. Rick Scott sues Booz Allen Hamilton over leaked tax returns
description: Sen. Rick Scott (R-Fla.) is suing Booz Allen Hamilton in federal court after his tax returns were leaked along with those of other wealthy Americans, including President Trump and Elon Musk.Why it matters: The tech and management consulting firm is already facing retribution from the Trump administration, which cancelled all of its contracts with the Treasury Department in January.Charles Littlejohn, who performed IRS contract work through Booz A

title: Images Show B-2 Spirit Bombers Departing for Iran Mission with Unknown White Patches on Wings
description: Images released by CENTCOM show B-2 stealth bombers preparing to launch for a mission over Iran last week, with multiple unidentified white patches added along the leading edges of their wings. The U.S. Central Command has released new photos of the B-2A Spirit stealth bomber, one of the assets that, albeit constantly flying as part []

title: USS Gerald R. Ford docks in Crete for maintenance after Red Sea fire
description:
The USS Gerald R. Ford (CVN 78) arrived at Naval Support Activity Souda Bay on the Greek island of Crete on 23 March 2026 to undergo maintenance and repairs after operating in the Red Sea.
The post USS Gerald R. Ford docks in Crete for maintenance after Red Sea fire appeared first on Naval Technology.

title: Generation Jihad | The regime falls when the people rise
description:
The bombs are falling. The Iranian regime is reeling. But revolutions aren’t won from 30,000 feet. As Washington and Jerusalem pummel toward a mission accomplished, Bill Roggio is joined by FDD's Jon Schanzer to grapple with a harder question that looms: what happens if there’s no uprising when the bombing stops?
The post Generation Jihad | The regime falls when the people rise appeared first on FDDs Long War Journal.
